user_query:
I need a 1,200–1,600-word structured mini literature review on transformer architectures for univariate and multivariate time series forecasting in business applications (e.g., demand planning, energy load, and traffic). Read the materials under input/:
- input/brief.md for scope, inclusion criteria, and guiding questions
- input/allowed_sources.csv for the only sources you are allowed to cite

Requirements:
1) Use only sources listed in input/allowed_sources.csv (no others) and cite at least 5 distinct sources.
2) Produce Markdown wrapped inside <ama-doc> ... </ama-doc> tags.
3) Include these sections with clear H2 headings: Introduction; Methodology (search and selection); Thematic Synthesis; Comparative Analysis; Open Challenges & Research Gaps; Conclusion; References.
4) In Comparative Analysis, include one Markdown table comparing at least four models across relevant attributes (e.g., key idea, complexity, strengths, limitations, reported datasets).
5) Include one Mermaid diagram that maps models to forecasting horizons (short-, medium-, long-term) and data characteristics (univariate vs. multivariate; stationary vs. non-stationary).
6) Use numbered in-text citations [1], [2], ... starting from [1] in increasing, contiguous order. Every cited number must appear exactly once in the References list, and every Reference must be cited in the text.
7) In the References section, provide entries with authors, year, title, venue, and URL exactly as they appear in input/allowed_sources.csv.
8) Focus on practical implications for practitioners while maintaining academic tone and rigor.

Deliverables:
- Write the review to output/literature_review.md.
- Create a machine-readable mapping in output/citations_used.csv with headers: citation_number,id — where id matches the 'id' field in input/allowed_sources.csv for each citation number used. Sort rows by citation_number ascending.
